title: Bounded long-session runtime
goal: Measure and bound worker output, telemetry, polling, economics persistence, and child-process supervision so 1/3/10-worker sessions have explicit resource behavior without premature infrastructure expansion.
acceptance: Use behavioral TDD: first add deterministic high-volume/quiet-worker/process-tree regressions and record baseline 1/3/10-worker measurements, then introduce bounds and rerun the same measurements.

Worker and shell monitors keep bounded in-memory tails/counters. Full raw output, when configured/needed, streams to append-only attempt artifacts rather than being retained and repeatedly cloned in memory; truncation/spill metadata is explicit.

Pi line observations are coalesced by bounded time/byte thresholds into transactional activity updates while preserving liveness, important semantic events, final counters, and debuggability; SQLite is not opened/written once per ordinary output line.
Polling uses an explicit adaptive/backoff or notification policy with tested maximum attention/cancellation latency, avoiding unconditional 500ms full-state scans for every quiet long-running worker.
Economics are maintained incrementally from typed counters/events and persisted atomically at bounded checkpoints; growing snapshots are not fully rescanned and rewritten for every observation.

Pi workers, verification commands, repair workers, and cancellable Git subprocesses share one deep child-process supervisor policy for process groups, timeout, cancellation, grace, escalation, output bounds, and terminal capture, with platform fallback documented.

Configuration exposes safe defaults and hard maximums for retained bytes/lines, observation flush cadence, polling/backoff, and raw-output spill; zero/unbounded values are deliberate and validated rather than accidental.

A reproducible local soak script and evidence note compare baseline and final wall time, peak RSS, SQLite row/write growth, artifact bytes, poll count, command count, and agent calls for 1, 3, and 10 deterministic workers; conclusions justify the chosen bounds and do not add pooling/pub-sub without evidence.
Tests cover multi-megabyte output, no-output workers, split UTF-8/JSON lines, output spill failure, cancellation with grandchildren, timeout races, and final economics/status agreement.
must_ask_if: Meeting the bound would require dropping authoritative worker results, questions, terminal events, or verification evidence.
The implementation would add an external telemetry system, database pool, or pub/sub broker before soak evidence shows it is necessary.
A default hard limit would silently kill valid workers instead of bounding observation/storage behavior.
Cross-platform process supervision would require removing the existing Unix safety guarantee.
